raw_title: Distributed Leadership in Schools as a Communication Process: A Systematic Review of Organizational Communication Dynamics and Leadership Capacity Building
raw_abstract: This study aims to examine distributed leadership in schools as a communication-driven process, focusing on how communication dynamics shape the development of leadership capacity and influence teachers’ perceptions of school improvement. Drawing on Spillane’s distributed leadership theory and perspectives from organizational communication, the study conceptualizes leadership as an interactive, meaning-making process embedded in everyday communicative practices. A mediated analytical framework was employed using structural equation modeling to explore the relationships among distributed leadership, communication processes, leadership capacity, and perceptions of school improvement. Data were collected from 312 teachers across 28 public schools. The findings indicate that distributed leadership, as enacted through communication practices, is positively associated with leadership capacity (β = .72, p < .001), while leadership capacity significantly predicts teachers’ perceptions of school improvement (β = .60, p < .001). Furthermore, leadership capacity partially mediates the relationship between distributed leadership and school improvement perceptions (indirect effect β = .43, p < .001), accounting for 36% of the variance. These results suggest that leadership effectiveness in schools is closely tied to communicative processes that enable collaboration, coordination, and shared understanding. The study contributes to the reconceptualization of leadership from a hierarchical model to a networked communication process and highlights the critical role of organizational communication in building leadership capacity for sustainable school improvement.
title_en: Distributed Leadership in Schools as a Communication Process: A Systematic Review of Organizational Communication Dynamics and Leadership Capacity Building
title_zh: 学校分布式领导作为一种沟通过程：组织沟通动态与领导力建设的系统综述
abstract_en: This study aims to examine distributed leadership in schools as a communication-driven process, focusing on how communication dynamics shape the development of leadership capacity and influence teachers’ perceptions of school improvement. Drawing on Spillane’s distributed leadership theory and perspectives from organizational communication, the study conceptualizes leadership as an interactive, meaning-making process embedded in everyday communicative practices. A mediated analytical framework was employed using structural equation modeling to explore the relationships among distributed leadership, communication processes, leadership capacity, and perceptions of school improvement. Data were collected from 312 teachers across 28 public schools. The findings indicate that distributed leadership, as enacted through communication practices, is positively associated with leadership capacity (β = .72, p < .001), while leadership capacity significantly predicts teachers’ perceptions of school improvement (β = .60, p < .001). Furthermore, leadership capacity partially mediates the relationship between distributed leadership and school improvement perceptions (indirect effect β = .43, p < .001), accounting for 36% of the variance. These results suggest that leadership effectiveness in schools is closely tied to communicative processes that enable collaboration, coordination, and shared understanding. The study contributes to the reconceptualization of leadership from a hierarchical model to a networked communication process and highlights the critical role of organizational communication in building leadership capacity for sustainable school improvement.
abstract_zh: 本研究旨在探讨学校中的分布式领导作为一种沟通驱动的过程，重点关注沟通动态如何塑造领导力的发展并影响教师对学校改进的看法。借鉴Spillane的分布式领导理论和组织沟通视角，本研究将领导力概念化为嵌入在日常沟通实践中的互动意义建构过程。采用结构方程模型的中介分析框架，探讨分布式领导、沟通过程、领导力和学校改进看法之间的关系。数据来自28所公立学校的312名教师。结果表明，通过沟通实践实现的分布式领导与领导力呈正相关（β = .72, p < .001），而领导力显著预测教师对学校改进的看法（β = .60, p < .001）。此外，领导力部分中介了分布式领导与学校改进看法之间的关系（间接效应β = .43, p < .001），解释了36%的方差。这些结果表明，学校的领导效能与促进协作、协调和共享理解的沟通过程密切相关。本研究有助于将领导力从层级模型重新概念化为网络化沟通过程，并强调组织沟通在建设可持续学校改进领导力中的关键作用。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了学校领导力如何通过日常沟通来发挥作用。研究发现，当领导力在教师之间分散并依靠沟通协作时，学校的领导能力会更强，教师也更认为学校在进步。研究使用了问卷调查和统计方法，分析了312名教师的反馈。简单来说，好的沟通能帮助学校领导力提升，从而推动学校变得更好。
plain_summary_en: This study looks at how school leadership works through everyday communication. It found that when leadership is shared among teachers and relies on communication and teamwork, schools build stronger leadership capacity, and teachers feel the school is improving. The research used surveys and statistics to analyze feedback from 312 teachers. In simple terms, good communication helps build leadership and makes schools better.
research_direction_zh: 组织沟通与分布式领导力
